strengths: ~17 years of continuous software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — meets the 15-year minimum with breadth across stack layers
Entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, still active), and startup experience at WatchUp where they shipped a top-10 Apple TV app
Current role at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling, showing recent, hands-on engagement with agentic AI development
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and recent: no ML/AI in the skills section, no AI products built at scale, and the only AI work cited is MCP code generators from April 2025 onward — insufficient for a role requiring recognized AI expertise with demonstrated platforms

No advanced degree: the role prefers a Master's or PhD in an AI-related field; candidate holds a BS in Computer Science and a BA in Photography/Fine Arts — no signal of graduate-level AI study or research

No evidence of director-level IC scope: no strategic customer advisory work, no public AI thought leadership (talks are on GIS and testing), and no track record influencing cross-organizational engineering strategy at the scale this role demands
response.justification: The role's two non-negotiable competencies are deep AI expertise (preferably with shipped AI platforms) and director-level strategic influence. Against AI depth, the resume offers only five weeks of MCP tooling work and no ML background — the skills list does not mention AI, ML, or any AI framework. Against strategic advisory scope, the candidate's most senior titles are 'Staff Software Engineer,' with no evidence of advising enterprise customers, setting AI strategy, or publishing AI research. The entrepreneurial signal (GISCollective, WatchUp) is genuine but pre-AI and insufficient to compensate. Experience years meet the floor, but the profile maps to a strong generalist IC, not a director-level AI strategist.
